title: Pinwheel black-border/seam: compositor mask-rasterization coverage (masks.ts)
goal: Movements__Pinwheel sits at 13.8 dB. DECODED (T-qfcdfc30f): the remaining gap is NOT an evaluator rig/rotation/flip issue — it is a compositor mask-rasterization COVERAGE bug in engine/src/compositor/masks.ts (resolveImageMaskAlpha, positioned/folded image-media-driver branch). Evidence: (1) engine renders a ~33px BLACK BORDER on all 4 frame edges at EVERY frame incl f0 where GT is 0% black (eng f0 black=6.8%, GT=0.0%; center scanline dark ONLY at x[0-32] and x[1887-1919]); (2) the 17 tile worldTransforms are CORRECT — projecting each square_fix driver's dropZone quad (576x576 x scale 1.35) through its worldTransform at t=0 gives screen bboxes whose UNION covers 100% of 1920x1080 (0.0000 uncovered); square_fix.png is a DISC of radius 288 inscribed in the 576 square (alpha>128 max radius=288.0, rounded transparent corners), and the scaled discs (r=389, centers ~290px apart) also cover 100% (0.0000 uncovered); (3) fold SENSE has ZERO render effect (negating rotX or rotY of the drivers is byte-identical — a center-anchored square tile folding +-pi/2 has a symmetric silhouette); (4) the A->B clone flip already fires at ~pi/2 edge-on (Clone A 10008 opacity 1->0 between t=0.43 and 0.57 for the [0,0.97] X-tile fold). Per-frame sim (fill engine black with GT) shows +1.6 to +5.3 dB/frame (f16 +5.3), ~+3 dB mean -> reaches the ~17 dB target. FIX in resolveImageMaskAlpha: the projected-quad rasterizer's alpha must reach the frame border (barycentric-UV alpha sampling likely drops the outer band, or per-group composite clips to each group's own bounds leaving border/inter-disc black). masks.ts/compositor are OUTSIDE the evaluator-only scope of T-qfcdfc30f.
